name: Enterprise Dense
description: A high-information-density style built for power users who spend hours in a tool. It prioritises getting maximum relevant data and controls on screen with compact spacing, tight rows, persistent navigation, and predictable conventions over visual flourish.
emotional_impression: Capable, serious, efficient, and built for work rather than delight.
visual_principles: Compact spacing scale and small-to-medium type sizes to maximise data per viewport
Tabular data, persistent toolbars, and multi-pane layouts
Conservative, conventional component patterns that reward muscle memory
Functional colour used semantically (status, severity) rather than decoratively
Clear, dense hierarchy with strong alignment and consistent column rhythm
accessibility_risks: Small touch and click targets can violate target-size guidance
Dense rows reduce readability and increase mis-click risk
Tight contrast budgets when fitting many states into limited space
performance_risks: Large tables and live grids require virtualisation to avoid jank
appropriate_contexts: Professional tools where users value information throughput over aesthetics
Workflows requiring side-by-side comparison of many records
Domains where users are trained and return daily
inappropriate_contexts: Consumer onboarding or first-run experiences for novices
Mobile-first products where density does not fit small screens
evidence: Bloomberg Terminal and enterprise admin consoles demonstrate sustained demand for high-density interfaces among trained users
WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) sets a 24x24 CSS px floor that dense UIs frequently approach
